---
title: "CMS Synthetic datasets"
author: "CMS"
date: "2008-01-01"
categories: [Data Management, Synthetic, National]
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(Statamarkdown)
stataexe <- "/Applications/StataNow/StataSE.app/Contents/MacOS/stata-se"
knitr::opts_chunk$set(engine.path=list(stata=stataexe))
```

@sec-example proves an example dataset for download.

## Source 

CMS has started to provide sythetic datasets so that people can familiarize themselves working with CMS data.  

On 2/12/2013 CMS released the Data Entrepreneurs’ Synthetic Public Use File (DE-SynPUF). The file contains synthesized data taken from a 5% random sample of Medicare beneficiaries in 2008 and their claims from 2008 to 2010 and preserve the detailed data structure and metadata of key variables at both the beneficiary and claim levels.

The DE-SynPUF was a joint project of NORC at the University of Chicago and IMPAQ International, a Maryland-based research, evaluation and technical assistance.  All variables in the DE-SynPUF are imputed, suppressed, and coarsened as part of disclosure treatment.

To date, this is still the only release available

## Dataset

Available at [this CMS website](https://www.cms.gov/data-research/statistics-trends-and-reports/medicare-claims-synthetic-public-use-files/cms-2008-2010-data-entrepreneurs-synthetic-public-use-file-de-synpuf)


## Dataset description

Contains multiple separate files.

[Data documentation](https://www.cms.gov/research-statistics-data-and-systems/downloadable-public-use-files/synpufs/downloads/synpuf_dug.pdf)

Represents a 5% sample of Medicare beneficiaries.

* Three years of claims (__2008__, __2009__, and __2010__) for the beneficiaries 
* Four types of claims (__Inpatient__, __Outpatient__, __Carrier__, __PDE__ – Prescription Drug Event) 
* A __beneficiary summary file__ for each year.


Due to file size limitations, each data type in the CMS Linkable 2008-2010 Medicare DE-SynPUF is released in 20 separate samples (essentially each is a .25% sample of all Medicare enrollees).  All claims for a particular beneficiary are in samples with the same number (i.e. all beneficiaries in sample 1 have all their claims in the sample 1 files).  This design allows DE-SynPUF users who do not need the entire synthetic population of the DE-SynPUF to read in only as many samples as they desire.  

__Sample 1 files__  (.csv files)

DE1.0 Sample 1 2008 Beneficiary Summary File (ZIP)  
DE1.0 Sample 1 2008-2010 Carrier Claims 1  
DE1.0 Sample 1 2008-2010 Carrier Claims 2  
DE1.0 Sample 1 2008-2010 Inpatient Claims (ZIP)  
DE1.0 Sample 1 2008-2010 Outpatient Claims (ZIP)  
DE1.0 Sample 1 2008-2010 Prescription Drug Events  
DE1.0 Sample 1 2009 Beneficiary Summary File (ZIP)  
DE1.0 Sample 1 2010 Beneficiary Summary File (ZIP)

### Features

| DE-SynPUF | Unit of record | Number of Records 2008 | Number of Records 2009 | Number of Records 2010 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Beneficiary Summary | Beneficiary | 2,326,856 | 2,291,320 | 2,255,098 |
| Inpatient Claims | claim | 547,800 | 504,941 | 280,081 |
| Outpatient Claims | claim | 5,673,808 | 6,519,340 | 3,633,839 |
| Carrier Claims | claim | 34,276,324 | 37,304,993 | 23,282,135 |
| Prescription Drug Events (PDE) | event | 39,927,827 | 43,379,293 | 27,778,849 |
Note: Claim counts for 2010 are lower due to attrition from death, and some effects of disclosure treatment.


### Variables

Vary by file

### Example {#sec-example}

Dataset with the entire population of benficiaries in the sample (2,326,705) and several variables.

Download: [cms_oop_costs.dta](../../data/cms_oop_costs.dta)  (large 34MB)

or use directly in code

```stata
use https://tphofer.github.io/ncsp-hhcr/data/cms_oop_costs.dta
```

Code creating the dataset from the 20 separate files of the beneficiary summary file for 2008 (each with about 116,000 records) is below. The files were hosted as csv files (rather than zipped files as on the CMS webstie) on a GitHub user's site.
```{stata,echo=1:18}
*| eval: false
  clear all
  frames reset
  frame create master
  local base "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GauriSaran/Inferential-Statistics-Project-Medicare-Claims/refs/heads/master/DE1_0_2008_Beneficiary_Summary_File_Sample"

  forvalues i = 1/20 {
      import delimited "`base'_`i'.csv", varnames(1) clear
      keep bene_sex_ident_cd sp_chf sp_cncr benres_ip benres_op benres_car
      rename bene_sex_ident_cd sex
      rename sp_chf heart_failure
	  rename sp_cncr cancer
    cwf master
    fframeappend, using(default)
    cwf default
  }

  frame change master
  egen total=rowtotal(benres_ip benres_op benres_car)
  label var total "Total out of pocket costs (excl. Part D)"
  save ../data/cms_oop_costs.dta, replace
```


#### variables

```stata
. codebook, compact

Variable          Obs Unique      Mean  Min    Max  Label
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
sex           2326705      2  1.555653    1      2  BENE_SEX_IDENT_CD
heart_fail~e  2326705      2   1.71546    1      2  SP_CHF
cancer        2326705      2  1.936386    1      2  SP_CNCR
benres_ip     2326705    423  247.2868    0  53096  BENRES_IP
benres_op     2326705   1174  197.5022    0  18700  BENRES_OP
benres_car    2326705    506  328.8734    0   5900  BENRES_CAR
total         2326705   6709  773.6624    0  54146  Total out of pocket costs (excl. Part D)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------


```

Note:  
__benres_ip__: Captures all inpatient hospitalizations, but it also includes Skilled Nursing Facility (SNF) and Hospice cost-sharing.  
__benres_op__: Captures hospital outpatient departments, ER visits, and Home Health Agency (HHA) institutional outpatient claims.  
__benres_car__: Captures non-institutional professional services, such as doctor office visits, lab tests, and Durable Medical Equipment (DME).



<!--  extra code
* codebook https://www.cms.gov/files/document/de-10-codebook.pdf-0
* https://github.com/GauriSaran/Inferential-Statistics-Project-Medicare-Claims/tree/master
* ADD in Part D

clear all
frames reset
frame create master
frame create pde // New frame specifically to hold aggregated Part D data

* --- PART 1: Loop for Beneficiary Summary Files (Your Original Structure) ---
local base_bsf "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GauriSaran/Inferential-Statistics-Project-Medicare-Claims/refs/heads/master/DE1_0_2008_Beneficiary_Summary_File_Sample"

forvalues i = 1/20 {
    import delimited "`base_bsf'_`i'.csv", varnames(1) clear
    keep desynpuf_id bene_sex_ident_cd sp_chf sp_cncr benres_ip benres_op benres_car
    rename bene_sex_ident_cd sex
    rename sp_chf heart_failure
    rename sp_cncr cancer
    cwf master
    fframeappend, using(default)
    cwf default
}

* --- PART 2: Loop for Prescription Drug Event (PDE) Files ---
* NOT HOSTED ON Gauri's site, so this is a placeholder URL. You will need to replace it with the actual location of the PDE sample files.
* BUT PART D IS MUCH LARGER, and costs are at the event level not the claim level, so we will sum up costs for each beneficiary in a separate frame before merging to master
* Note: Replace this URL with the exact repository path to Gauri's Part D/PDE sample files if different
local base_pde "https://githubusercontent.com"

cwf pde
forvalues i = 1/20 {
    * Note: capture confirms execution if certain samples are missing drug records
    capture import delimited "`base_pde'_`i'.csv", varnames(1) clear
    if _rc == 0 {
        keep desynpuf_id ptnt_pay_amt
        cwf pde
        fframeappend, using(default)
        cwf default
    }
}

* --- PART 3: Aggregate and Merge the Datasets ---
cwf pde
* Sum up every transaction a single person made into an annual drug total
collapse (sum) benres_ptext=ptnt_pay_amt, by(desynpuf_id)

* Switch to master frame to link the aggregated data
cwf master
frlink 1:1 desynpuf_id, frame(pde)
frget benres_rx = benres_ptext, from(pde)

* Clean up missing values to 0 for beneficiaries who didn't fill prescriptions 
recode benres_rx (. = 0)

* --- PART 4: Calculate True Covered Out-of-Pocket Expenses ---
egen total = rowtotal(benres_ip benres_op benres_car benres_rx)


* SIMPLE
clear all
  tempfile master
  save `master', emptyok

  local base "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GauriSaran/Inferential-Statistics-Project-Medicare-Claims/refs/heads/master/DE1_0_2008_Beneficiary_Summary_File_Sample"

  forvalues i = 1/20 {
      import delimited "`base'_`i'.csv", varnames(1) clear
      keep bene_sex_ident_cd sp_chf
      rename bene_sex_ident_cd sex
      rename sp_chf heart_failure
      append using `master'
      save `master', replace
  }

  use `master', clear
  
* Bulk download, only  if needed (missing from directory) using curl xargs.

clear all

  local base "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/GauriSaran/Inferential-Statistics-Project-Medicare-Claims/refs/heads/master/DE1_0_2008_Beneficiary_Su
  mmary_File_Sample"
  local dir "medicare_csvs"

  capture mkdir "`dir'"

  // Parallel download — skip if all 20 files are already cached locally
  local need_download 0
  forvalues i = 1/20 {
      capture confirm file "`dir'/sample_`i'.csv"
      if _rc local need_download 1
  }
  if `need_download' {
      shell seq 1 20 | xargs -I{} -P 8 curl -fsSL -o "`dir'/sample_{}.csv" "`base'_{}.csv"
  }

  // Sequential import + append from local files
  tempfile master
  save `master', emptyok

  forvalues i = 1/20 {
      import delimited "`dir'/sample_`i'.csv", varnames(1) clear
      keep bene_sex_ident_cd sp_chf
      rename bene_sex_ident_cd sex
      rename sp_chf heart_failure
      append using `master'
      save `master', replace
  }

  use `master', clear

-->
